claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period; Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
circumstances: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let's start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - my son Alex. He's 18 years old and lives with me. I wanted to find out if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I can see you have an 18-year-old son named Alex who lives with you.

Since Alex is 18, a key factor for Child Benefit eligibility is whether he's still in approved education or training. Child Benefit can continue for children under 20 if they remain in approved education or training.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training (such as A-levels, further education college, or an approved training course)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in education anymore. He left school and he's working now - he has a full-time job at a local warehouse.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I have a follow-up question to fully assess Alex's eligibility:

**How many hours per week does Alex work at his warehouse job?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He works full-time - it's over 24 hours a week.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all the information I needed. 

**Based on your circumstances, I'm afraid you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why: While Child Benefit can normally continue for children up to age 20 if they remain in approved education or training, you stop being eligible if your child starts paid work for 24 hours or more per week and is no longer in approved education or training.

Since Alex is 18, has left education, and is working full-time (over 24 hours per week) at the warehouse, you are no longer entitled to receive Child Benefit for him.

If Alex's circumstances change in the future (for example, if he returns to approved education or training and reduces his working hours), you may be able to claim again at that time.

Is there anything else I can help clarify?

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and has left education. According to the Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit for children under 16, or under 20 if they stay in approved education or training. However, you'll stop getting Child Benefit if your child starts paid work for 24 hours or more a week and is no longer in approved education or training. Since Alex is working full-time (over 24 hours per week) at a warehouse and is not in approved education or training, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for him.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 18 years old. Alex has left education and is working full-time (over 24 hours per week) at a warehouse. Under Child Benefit rules, eligibility stops when a child starts paid work for 24 hours or more per week and is no longer in approved education or training. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 18 years old and has left education. According to the Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit for children under 16, or under 20 if they stay in approved education or training. However, you'll stop getting Child Benefit if your child starts paid work for 24 hours or more a week and is no longer in approved education or training. Since Alex is working full-time (over 24 hours per week) at a warehouse and is not in approved education or training, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for him.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 18 years old. Alex has left education and is working full-time (over 24 hours per week) at a warehouse. Under Child Benefit rules, eligibility stops when a child starts paid work for 24 hours or more per week and is no longer in approved education or training. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
